summary: Tested the brand-new canonical Spades room (SpadesAAA) end-to-end. Backend pytest /app/backend/tests/test_spades_aaa.py passes 10/10. Cyber Casino regression test_cyber_casino.py passes 11/11 (1 skipped flow-conditional). Frontend Playwright walkthrough at /spades validated lobby (mode + ruleset toggles, House Rules, Start CTA, Back), AI-match phase transition, Big Wheel ruleset metadata (badge top-right + Big Wheel hint in bid wheel), 14 bid chips with cyan-highlighted suggested pill, hand fan with 13 cards rendered + spades-not-broken dimming visible, score panel (Your Team / Opponents / 200, bags 0/5), back-to-lobby navigation, Live mode → queue placeholder, and mobile portrait at 390px (no horizontal scroll: scrollWidth=clientWidth=390). Auth gating works: POST /api/spades-practice/start returns 401 without bearer; GET /api/spades/rulesets is correctly public.

issues: An EXPLORE/'What's new' platform-wide top banner overlaps the score panel + top-right Big Wheel/AI Practice badges on desktop. Cosmetic only — toggles still clickable. Consider letting the SpadesAAA top bar render above z-index 50 of that announcement banner.

action_items: (Low priority, testability): Hand-fan cards in SpadesHandFan render as motion.div with onClick — Playwright selector '[data-testid="spades-hand-fan"] button' returned 0 hits. Add role="button" + per-card data-testid="spades-card-{rank}-{suit}" so future automation can click cards directly. (Visual dimming of spades-not-broken cards already works correctly per screenshot.)

(Cosmetic): The platform-wide 'What's new… EXPLORE' announcement banner z-orders above the SpadesAAA top bar/score panel. Either dismiss-by-default on game routes or push SpadesAAA top bar to a higher z-index.

(Pre-existing, not Spades-related): Recurring console error 'TypeError: e is not a function' from bundle.js (Privy SDK init when CSP blocks the auth.privy.io iframe). Spams the console on every page including /spades. Worth a follow-up — does not break Spades functionality.
critical_code_review_comments: SpadesAAA.tsx is 519 lines — borderline large but cohesive (state machine + 3 phase renderers). Acceptable for now; if it crosses ~700 lines extract the AI-mode controller into a usePracticeGame hook.
All API URLs sourced from process.env.REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — good.
Backend StartRequest validates ruleset enum with explicit 400 — good (test_start_rejects_unknown_ruleset green).
test_spades_aaa.py uses fresh demo-login per test → fully isolated assertions, no shared state — good pattern, keep.
Live multiplayer mode is intentionally a placeholder (queue screen only) per the review request — flagged in PRD context, not a bug.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (lobby, AI start, bid, score panel, back nav, live queue placeholder, mobile responsive). Card-click-to-play not asserted via Playwright due to missing per-card testid; covered by backend test_full_trick_advances_state instead.

test_credentials: POST /api/auth/demo-login (no body) → returns session_token. Used as Bearer for all authenticated calls.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests provision fresh demo users per run; no DB pollution.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SpadesAAA canonical room is solid. Live multiplayer is a deliberate placeholder (queue screen only) — when main agent wires HTTP multiplayer queue→game, retest the live flow at /spades after clicking Find Opponents. The 6 legacy spades pages (SpadesGame, SpadesPractice, SpadesPremiumAAA, UndergroundSpades, HttpMultiplayerSpades, HttpMultiplayerSpades4P) are STILL routable by design — do not flag them as duplicates until the user signs off on SpadesAAA. Aliases /spades-aaa, /spades-aaa/:gameId, /spades/premium, /spades/premium/:gameId all wire to SpadesAAA per gamesRoutes.tsx (verified by code grep, not all clicked). REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL must be exported in the shell before running pytest, otherwise tests skip via conftest._env guard.
rca of the issue: No bugs found that block the feature. The only minor friction was a Playwright selector miss on hand-fan cards (they render as motion.div without role=button) — recommended adding per-card data-testid for future automation.
